'''David Hulme''' (born 6 February 1964) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], and at club level for [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], the [[Leeds Rhinos]] and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], as a {{rlp|so}}, or {{rlp|sh}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Early life==
Having started his playing career at St Bede's RC Junior School, David moved to St Joseph's RC Comprehensive in Widnes (where he played Centre for their RU team) whilst continuing playing Amateur Rugby League for {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/20120320233631/http://www.hfharlfc.btck.co.uk/HallOfFame Halton Hornets]}} in a set up that also produced future Widnes team mates [[Barry Dowd]] (1982–93) and [[Andy Currier]].

His first taste of the "limelight" was in the Widnes Under-11 team that won the "Curtain raiser" to the 1975 [[Challenge Cup]] Final, when Widnes played [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], and won the "Main Event" 14–7.<ref name="Widnes Challenge Cup winners of 1975 back at Wembley">{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/03/10/widnes-challenge-cup-winners-of-1975-back-at-wembley-55368-23106920|title=Widnes Challenge Cup winners of 1975 back at Wembley|publisher=runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk|date=31 December 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090706230344/http://www.runcornandwidnesweeklynews.co.uk/widnes-vikings/widnes-vikings-news/2009/03/10/widnes-challenge-cup-winners-of-1975-back-at-wembley-55368-23106920/|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=6 July 2009}}</ref>

==Playing career==

===Widnes===
Hulme played as a substitute in [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]' 19–6 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1984 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1983–84 Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1983–84 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 5 May 1984, in front of a crowd of 80,116.

Hulme played, and was [[player of the match|man of the match]] winning the [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] in [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]' 38-14 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[Rugby League Premiership|Premiership]] Final during the [[1987–88 Rugby Football League season|1987–88 season]] at [[Old Trafford]] on Sunday 15 May 1988. Hulme played {{rlp|sh}}, and was [[player of the match|man of the match]] in [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]' 30–18 victory over [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[1989 World Club Challenge]] at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]] on Wednesday 4 October 1989. Hulme played {{rlp|sh}} in [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]' 24-18 victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the [[1990 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] at [[Central Park (Wigan)|Central Park]], [[Wigan]] on Saturday 29 September 1990. Hulme played {{rlp|sh}} in [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]' 6-12 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1988–89 League Cup (rugby league)|1988–89 John Player Special Trophy]] Final during the [[1988–89 Rugby Football League season|1988–89 season]] at [[Burnden Park]], [[Bolton]] on Saturday 7 January 1989.<ref name="7th January 1989: Wigan 12 Widnes 6 (John Player Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2756|title=7th January 1989: Wigan 12 Widnes 6 (John Player Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-date=18 May 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150518082424/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2756|url-status=dead}}</ref> Hulme's [[Testimonial match]] at Widnes took place in 1991.

===Leeds===
Hulme joined [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in 1996, making nine appearances and scoring two tries for the club in [[Super League I]].<ref>{{cite web|title=David Hulme|url=http://www.therhinos.co.uk/rugby/matchcentre/leeds_rhinos_1st_xiii_profiles.php?player=84027&includeref=dynamic|publisher=Leeds Rhinos|access-date=18 February 2014|archive-url=https://archive.today/20140218170552/http://www.therhinos.co.uk/rugby/matchcentre/leeds_rhinos_1st_xiii_profiles.php?player=84027&includeref=dynamic|archive-date=18 February 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===International honours===
Hulme won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at Widnes in 1988 against Papua New Guinea, Australia (3 matches), and New Zealand, and in 1989 against New Zealand (3 matches).<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Hulme was selected to go on the [[1988 Great Britain Lions tour]].

==Genealogical information==
Hulme is the older brother of the rugby league footballer, [[Paul Hulme]], and is the father of the rugby league footballer [[Danny Hulme]] who scored the fastest hat trick of tries for Widnes Vikings against Halifax in 2011.
